This article details the [[Bradford Bulls]] rugby league football club's 2023 season. This is the Bulls' fifth consecutive season in the [[Championship (rugby league)|Championship]].

==Season review==

===July 2022===

Loose forward [[Ben Evans (rugby league)|Ben Evans]] signed a two-year deal with [[Barrow Raiders]] for next season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/sportlatest/20233725.bradford-forward-evans-join-barrow-2023/|title=Bradford forward Evans to join Barrow from 2023 |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=24 June 2022 |accessdate=10 September 2022}}</ref> Prop [[Daniel Fleming (rugby league)|Daniel Fleming]] left the Bulls to join promotion chasing [[Featherstone Rovers]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/sportlatest/20292612.fleming-leaves-bradford-bulls-sign-featherstone/|title=Fleming leaves Bradford Bulls to sign for Featherstone |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=20 July 2022 |accessdate=10 September 2022}}</ref>

===August 2022===

On loan second row forward [[Chester Butler (rugby league)|Chester Butler]] became the first signing of the 2023 season as it was announced that he would leave the [[Huddersfield Giants]] to sign a two-year deal at the Bulls.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/sportlatest/20674443.chester-butler-makes-bradford-bulls-move-permanent/|title=Chester Butler makes Bradford Bulls move permanent |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=19 August 2022 |accessdate=10 September 2022}}</ref> The second signing of the season came in the form of experienced [[RFL Championship]] winger [[Ben Blackmore]] from [[Dewsbury Rams]] on a one-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/sportlatest/20779863.bradford-bulls-sign-former-sheffield-eagles-winger-blackmore/|title=Bradford Bulls sign former Sheffield Eagles winger Blackmore |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=26 August 2022 |accessdate=10 September 2022}}</ref> Halfback [[Tom Holmes (rugby league)|Tom Holmes]] from [[Featherstone Rovers]] became the third signing for the 2023 season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.examinerlive.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/bradford-bulls-tom-holmes-sheffield-24879225|title=Bradford Bulls land deal for leading Championship halfback |work=Examiner Live |date=29 August 2022 |accessdate=10 September 2022}}</ref>

Championship]] side [[London Broncos]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/sportlatest/21223069.bradford-bulls-sign-brad-foster-london-broncos/|title=Bradford Bulls sign ===January 2023===

To start the new year off the Bulls announced the signing of centre [[Joe Arundel]] following his departure from [[Halifax Panthers]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/sportlatest/23222702.bradford-bulls-sign-former-loan-star-halifax-spell/|title=Bradford Bulls sign former loan star after Halifax spell |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=1 January 2023 |accessdate=3 January 2023}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bradfordbulls.co.uk/article/1768/arundel-is-a-bull|title=ARUNDEL IS A BULL |work=Bradford Bulls |date=1 January 2023 |accessdate=3 January 2023}}</ref> The 2023 squad numbers were revealed with notable numbers being taken, fullback [[Jack Walker (rugby league)|Jack Walker]] took the number 31 shirt (the one he debuted with for [[Leeds Rhinos]]) rather than the traditional number 1. Captain [[Michael Lawrence (rugby league)|Michael Lawrence]] will wear the number 10 shirt whilst [[George Flanagan]] would wear the number 9.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/sportlatest/23243486.bradford-bulls-squad-numbers-2023-revealed-last-night/|title=Bradford Bulls squad numbers for 2023 revealed last night |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=11 January 2023 |accessdate=27 January 2023}}</ref> Second rower [[Sam Scott (rugby league)|Sam Scott]] announced he was retiring immediately after failing to overcome a recurring injury.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/sportlatest/23247543.bradford-bulls-second-row-sam-scott-retires-due-injury/|title=Bradford Bulls second row Sam Scott retires due to injury |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=13 January 2023 |accessdate=27 January 2023}}</ref> Bradford started their pre-season with a convincing 34-16 win over [[Dewsbury Rams]], new signings [[Brad Foster (rugby league)|Brad Foster]], [[Joe Arundel]] and [[Jack Walker (rugby league)|Jack Walker]] all crossed for tries whilst young half [[Myles Lawford]] kicked five goals as well as scoring a try himself.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bradfordbulls.co.uk/article/1787/bulls-open-2023-with-six-try-success|title=BULLS OPEN 2023 WITH SIX-TRY SUCCESS |work=Bradford Bulls |date=16 January 2023 |accessdate=8 February 2023}}</ref> The scheduled friendly against [[Huddersfield Giants]] was cancelled due to a frozen pitch.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.giantsrl.com/article/8345/bradford-friendly-called-off|title=BRADFORD FRIENDLY CALLED OFF! |work=Huddersfield Giants |date=22 January 2023 |accessdate=8 February 2023}}</ref> After the departure of Desmond-Walker and the retirement of Scott, Bradford bolstered their pack by signing experienced forward [[Josh Johnson (rugby league)|Josh Johnson]] from [[Widnes Vikings]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/sportlatest/23282281.bradford-bulls-sign-former-widnes-salford-prop-josh-johnson/|title=Bradford Bulls sign former Widnes and Salford prop Josh Johnson |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=27 January 2023 |accessdate=8 February 2023}}</ref> The Bulls finished pre-season with a 24-10 loss to local rivals [[Leeds Rhinos]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/sportlatest/23284405.bradford-bulls-battle-hard-narrow-friendly-defeat-leeds-rhinos/|title=Bradford Bulls battle hard in narrow friendly defeat to Leeds Rhinos |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=29 January 2023 |accessdate=8 February 2023}}</ref>

===February 2023===

The Bulls began the [[2023 RFL Championship]] season with a comfortable 24-8 win over [[Whitehaven R.L.F.C.]], last years top try scorer [[Kieran Gill]] got off the mark with two tries whilst [[Chester Butler (rugby league)|Chester Butler]] and [[George Flanagan]] both scored tries.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/sportlatest/23300802.bradford-bulls-break-whitehaven-curse-get-opening-day-win/|title=Bradford Bulls break Whitehaven curse to get opening day win |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=5 February 2023 |accessdate=20 February 2023}}</ref> Following the positive start to the season, the Bulls were brought back down to earth with a 32-16 loss to [[York Knights]], winger [[David Foggin-Johnston]], [[Jordan Lilley]] and vice captain [[Bodene Thompson]] all scored however it was not enough to prevent the Bulls first defeat of the season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/sportlatest/23324843.bradford-bulls-captain-blasts-sides-attitude-approach-york/|title=Bradford Bulls captain blasts side's attitude and approach at York |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=16 February 2023 |accessdate=20 February 2023}}</ref> Prop forward [[Masi Matongo]] was announced as the latest signing for the Bulls following a successful trial period from [[York Knights]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/sportlatest/23330312.squad-bradford-bulls-confirm-masi-matongo-return-captain-missing/|title=SQUAD: Bradford Bulls confirm Masi Matongo return but captain missing |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=17 February 2023 |accessdate=20 February 2023}}</ref>
